heading: Brunswick Heads vs Brushy Creek — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Brunswick Heads, New South Wales and Brushy Creek, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Brunswick Heads has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Brushy Creek (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Brunswick Heads or Brushy Creek safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Brunswick Heads has a flood risk score of 26/100, while Brushy Creek scores 28/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Brunswick Heads scores 36/100 for bushfire risk. Brushy Creek scores 60/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Brunswick Heads or Brushy Creek?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Brunswick Heads has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
